blocks.text: Post-Merger Integration: Carroll, Merritt and Williams

items: Client of record: {{fact:f:E-2009-003.client}}
Engagement commenced {{fact:f:E-2009-003.start}}
Prepared for Joseph Wallace, Chief Financial Officer
Prepared by Daniel Leach, with Jason Torres and Mary Parker
Status briefing, early phase of the engagement
A single reconciled view of the combined company as it stands today
A cost baseline for the combined business, built from your own management accounts
A target operating model for the functions in scope
A prioritized plan for the first period after integration
Findings and recommendations you can act on within the engagement term
Findings first: every figure ties back to a workpaper we can produce on request
Combined and legacy views shown side by side, not blended into a single number
Interim results brought to you as they firm up, not held to the end
Findings and recommendations kept separate and clearly labeled
Headcount and reporting data reconciled across both legacy companies by Mary Parker
Functional overlaps being mapped by Jason Torres and Daniel Leach
Cost baseline underway; the acquired-company accounts are now in hand
Overhead is defined differently by each company and needs to be settled
No findings yet: this is an early status, not a result
Confirmation of the in-scope function list
Sign-off from your finance staff on the account definitions
Access to the people who can answer questions on the acquired-company accounts
Close the cost baseline once the account definitions are confirmed
Complete the functional overlap map for the in-scope functions
Bring the first findings to the next working session, labeled as findings
Continue routing requests through Joseph Wallace's office
